findings.headline: DREVEX EU — systematic threshold gaming with Obec Liptovske Sliace: 3 contracts totalling 677K EUR, one dodatok tuned to 2,216 EUR below EU limit

evidence.threshold_flag: Contract 11696268 (dodatok) at 212,783.85 EUR is only 2,216 EUR below the 215K EU procurement threshold
evidence.splitting_pattern: Two separate kupne zmluvy signed 9 days apart (Sep 24 + Oct 3) to the same buyer for nearly identical subject matter (both titled 'Kupna zmluva uzatvorena v sulade so zakonom c. 343-2015 Z.z.'). Combined value 464,915.40 EUR — well above the 215K EU threshold that would require formal EU-level procurement.

why_not_innocent: This is NOT a standard municipal spending pattern — DREVEX accounts for 82.9% of all Obec Liptovske Sliace contract value (677K out of 817K total). No other supplier exceeds 80K EUR with this buyer.

Two near-identical purchase contracts signed 9 days apart suggest deliberate splitting to keep each individual contract from triggering enhanced scrutiny. Their combined value (464,915 EUR) is more than double the EU threshold.

The dodatok (amendment) to the first contract was priced at exactly 212,783.85 EUR — landing just 2,216 EUR below the 215K EU procurement limit. This precision is characteristic of threshold gaming, not natural pricing.
DREVEX is a micro-firm (3-4 employees) manufacturing furniture in Prievidza, contracting with a municipality 170+ km away in Liptov region. The geographic disconnect adds to suspicion.
The company posted a loss of -139,597 EUR in 2025 despite receiving 677K in contracts from this single buyer, raising questions about where the money went.
All 3 contracts lack UVO procurement URLs, meaning no public procurement record is linked — despite the combined value far exceeding thresholds.
next_steps: PDF review of all 3 contracts to verify exact scope — are they for the same type of furniture/goods?
UVO search for Obec Liptovske Sliace procurement announcements in Q3-Q4 2025
Check if the dodatok amount (212,783.85) represents a price reduction or new delivery — the title references the original Sep 24 contract

findings.headline: DREVEX EU — extreme buyer concentration: 74% of all contract revenue from single municipality
why_not_innocent: A micro-firm deriving 74% of its CRZ contract revenue from one small municipality is not a normal commercial relationship. This suggests a captive supplier-buyer arrangement.
Obec Liptovske Sliace has 27 distinct suppliers but 82.9% of its total spending goes to DREVEX — an extraordinary concentration for furniture procurement.
The next largest supplier (GV, s.r.o.) received only 79,792 EUR — less than 12% of what DREVEX received. The gap is extreme.
This is NOT explained by the municipality being a large institution — Obec Liptovske Sliace is a small village (population ~3,000) with total contract volume of 817K EUR.
next_steps: foaf.sk network mapping to check for personal connections between DREVEX UBO (Zdenka Krizanova) and Obec Liptovske Sliace officials
Check whether Obec Liptovske Sliace has received EU structural funds or grants that might explain large furniture purchases

findings.headline: DREVEX EU — single UBO (Zdenka Krizanova), RPVS registered but no cross-network connections detected

evidence.foaf_connections: not checked — no foaf data in network.json

why_inconclusive: RPVS shows Zdenka Krizanova as sole UBO — no multi-company network visible from RPVS alone.

The ex-offo deletion of the original RPVS entry (vlozka 7426, deleted Oct 2017) and re-registration (vlozka 17829) is a minor procedural flag but not necessarily suspicious — could be a technical correction.
Without foaf.sk data, we cannot check if Krizanova has roles in other companies or connections to Obec Liptovske Sliace representatives.
The UBO address (Diviacka Nova Ves) is near the company address (Prievidza) — consistent, not suspicious.
next_steps: foaf.sk lookup for Zdenka Krizanova — check for other company roles
Check Obec Liptovske Sliace elected officials (starosta, zastupitelstvo) for any connection to Krizanova family
Verify if SOSKA LEGAL s.r.o. (the RPVS verifier) has any other clients that also contract with Obec Liptovske Sliace

findings.headline: DREVEX EU — tax debtor operating at a loss while receiving 912K in public contracts

evidence.contradiction: FinStat shows 'vysoko spolahlivy' tax status but company appears in fs_tax_debtors register. Revenue of 347K in 2025 vs. 912K in CRZ contracts suggests significant portion of contract value passes through to subcontractors or is recognized in different periods.
evidence.loss_after_profit_streak: Company was profitable 2020-2024 (profit range 7K-160K) then swung to -139K loss in 2025 — the year it received 677K from Obec Liptovske Sliace

why_not_innocent: A company receiving 677K in furniture contracts from one buyer while reporting only 347K in total revenue suggests either (a) revenue recognition across fiscal years, (b) pass-through to subcontractors, or (c) accounting irregularities.
The swing from consistent profitability to a 139K loss in the same year as the largest contracts is counterintuitive — more revenue should mean more profit, not less, for a furniture supplier.
Being listed in fs_tax_debtors while holding 'vysoko spolahlivy' status creates a contradiction that warrants investigation.
next_steps: Review 2025 financial statements in RegisterUZ for detailed cost breakdown
Check if DREVEX uses subcontractors (LLM extractions unavailable for these contracts)
Verify the fs_tax_debtors entry — is it current or historical?

dismissed.pattern: Missing expiry dates on all 4 contracts
dismissed.reason: DISMISSED — 'missing_expiry' is an info-level flag. Purchase contracts (kupne zmluvy) for goods delivery commonly lack explicit expiry dates because they are fulfilled upon delivery. This is standard practice, not suspicious.

dismissed.pattern: Fourth contract (11938512) with different buyer (Centrum podpornych sluzieb Trnava) at 235,176 EUR
dismissed.reason: DISMISSED as cross-referencing concern — this contract is with a completely different buyer, shows DREVEX has at least some commercial activity outside Obec Liptovske Sliace. Amount above 215K is noted but this is a separate procurement by a different entity.

DISMISSED as standalone concern — ex-offo deletions in 2017 were common during the early phase of RPVS when many entries had technical deficiencies. The company re-registered and has maintained valid status since Aug 2017.

summary.narrative: DREVEX EU, s.r.o. (ICO 36835463) presents a clear pattern of threshold gaming with Obec Liptovske Sliace. A micro-firm with 3-4 employees received 677,699 EUR across 3 contracts in just 56 days (Sep-Nov 2025), representing 82.9% of the municipality's total contract spending. Two near-identical purchase contracts were signed 9 days apart, and a subsequent dodatok was priced at exactly 212,783.85 EUR — just 2,216 EUR below the EU procurement threshold. The combined value of any two of these contracts exceeds the 215K limit that would require formal EU-level procurement. The company's sole UBO is Zdenka Krizanova (via RPVS), but without foaf.sk data, potential connections to municipal officials remain unchecked. The financial picture is contradictory: 347K revenue reported vs. 912K in contracts, and a 139K loss in the year of highest contract activity. The company is listed as a tax debtor despite holding 'vysoko spolahlivy' tax status. Priority next steps: (1) PDF review to confirm contract splitting, (2) foaf.sk mapping of Krizanova and Obec officials, (3) UVO procurement search for the buyer.
